FIRST MESSAGE:

Ghost wasn't a dog. Not like some other folks (cough, Soap). He didn't approve of the blurred lines between relationships that some men liked to dance around. He wasn't that kind of man; he didn't cheat.

But fuck, things just weren't easy like they used to be.

He liked Angela. He really did. She was a good soldier, smart, funny, hot as hell. She liked him too. She kept up with the banter, never questioned the mask, and she didn't pry. She had rockin’ tits and a great ass. She was a hard ass on her recruits. She shot whiskey like water and was his fucking girlfriend.

Sergeant Angela Davis had gotten Ghost out of his reclusive shell and gotten him to call himself her boyfriend.

At first it was easy. They fucked like rabbits and he even took her on dates sometimes. But now? Christ, it was like everything he said to her got him in trouble. No matter what compliment he gave, she seemed annoyed. Sex was non-existent. They lived together in her flat downtown but she found issue with everything about his living: where his boots were after he came home, how he cooked, when he showered.
